steps.title: Use relative years
steps.content: Let $t$ be years after $1998$. Then $1998$ is $t=0$, $2018$ is $t=20$, and $2022$ is $t=24$. The known points are $(0,60)$ and $(20,224)$, with population measured in thousands.

steps.title: Evaluate the fitted model at 2022
steps.content: Find the slope from the two known points: $$m=\frac{224-60}{20-0}=\frac{164}{20}=8.2$$ Since the point with input $0$ has output $60$, the model is $y=8.2t+60$. For $2022$, plug in $t=24$: $$y=8.2(24)+60=256.8.$$ Rounded to the nearest whole number, this is $257$ thousand.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The requested value of $x$ is the rounded population in thousands, so $x=257$.
